title: FEMA climate whitewash — 2026-05-18
content:
Trump-appointed Review Council released a FEMA overhaul proposal, framed as modernization and efficiency improvement. Administration officials say the changes streamline disaster response and reduce bureaucratic friction.

## What the Data Says

- The 150+ page report contains exactly ONE mention of "climate"
- 2025 was the third-highest year for billion-dollar climate disasters in US history
- 23 separate billion-dollar events; 276 deaths; $115 billion in total damages
- FEMA was architecturally designed around climate-adjacent disaster response
- Any credible overhaul of a disaster agency that managed $115B in climate damages must address climate as a core variable
- Administration simultaneously withdrew from Paris Agreement and cutting climate agency capacity

## The Real Story

An agency that managed $115B in climate disaster costs is being "reformed" by a report that refuses to name the cause of the problem. The one-word climate mention in a 150+ page document is institutional denial made structural. The piece connects the damage toll, death count, and obvious conflict of interest to the administration's pattern of burying climate in its own agency reports.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: FEMA 'Modernization' Is a Liability Shield: The overhaul proposal eliminates civil climate liability pathways while retaining the same bureaucratic structure — that's not reform, that's immunizing the agency.
